LAW AND MOTION 301, PLAINTIFF KALED MORSEY'S AMENDED MOTION TO STAY CIVIL PROCEEDINGS. THE UNOPPOSED MOTION TO STAY THE CASE IN ITS ENTIRETY IS GRANTED, WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO A MOTION TO LIFT THE STAY BY ANY PARTY WHO HAS NOT YET APPEARED IN THIS CASE, AND WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO A MOTION TO LIFT THE STAY BASED ON CHANGED CIRCUMSTANCES. calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-21-2026 Continued To Dec-16-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion To Stay Civil Proceedings
Off Calendar Feb-18-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF KALED MORSEY'S Amended Motion To Stay Civil Proceedings.

The unopposed motion to stay the case in its entirety is granted, without prejudice to a motion to lift the stay by any party who has not yet appeared in this case, and without prejudice to a motion to lift the stay based on changed circumstances.

Proposed order submitted.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Stay Civil Action Pending Resolution Of Related Criminal Proceedings
Off Calendar Feb-10-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF KALED MORSEY's Motion To Stay Civil Action Pending Resolution Of Related Criminal Proceedings.

Continued for hearing to February 18, 2026 pursuant to plaintiff's amended notice of motion, filed on January 23, 2026.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)
